sourceMovie.title: Gosford Park
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Gosford Park works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Murder on the Orient Express
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gosford Park clicked for you, Murder on the Orient Express (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Gaslight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gosford Park clicked for you, Gaslight (1944) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Gosford Park?
faq.answer: Try Murder on the Orient Express, Gaslight, An Inspector Calls—each shares DNA with Gosford Park while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Gosford Park?
faq.answer: Gosford Park (2001) is best described as Drama, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Gosford Park worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
